author: Nia Chen
why: The team chose a small Flask prototype because the route shape was unknown and fast local iteration mattered more than permanent framework choice.

author: Mateo Ruiz
why: Redis was selected as the first shared session store because it was fast, easy to run locally, and had native TTL behavior for expiring inactive sessions.

author: Priya Shah
message: document redis persistence failures and alternatives
why: Redis restarts and memory pressure caused sessions to disappear without enough durable evidence for support; the team paused implementation to compare Redis AOF, Redis Streams, PostgreSQL, DynamoDB, and sticky sessions.

author: Owen Brooks
message: replace redis session store with postgres
why: Sessions had become product data, so PostgreSQL replaced Redis to provide transactions, durable rows, queryable support history, and clearer migration discipline.

author: Nia Chen
why: The team moved from Flask to FastAPI because typed request models and generated OpenAPI docs reduced schema drift after the session API became a supported contract.
